[observation] [info] CODEX.md alongside CLAUDE.md indicates dual AI-assistant support; keeping both files synchronized when architectural conventions change is a low-cost but important maintenance discipline.
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 1.0 with zero coupling is correct and expected for an orchestration root zone — all files belong together as project metadata and entry scripts with no import graph edges.
[observation] [info] The four entry points (cli.js, web.js, ci.js, config.js) enforce the spawn-only rule at the orchestration tier; any new root-level script should be evaluated against the tier boundary decision table in CLAUDE.md before being added here.

<insights>

- Contains the four orchestration-tier entry points that enforce the spawn-only tier boundary across all packages.
- High cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling is ideal for a root zone — these files are intentionally self-contained with no import relationships.

- The four entry points (cli.js, web.js, ci.js, config.js) enforce the spawn-only rule at the orchestration tier; any new root-level script should be evaluated against the tier boundary decision table in CLAUDE.md before being added here.
